recipes.title: Adobo
recipes.description: Filipino national dish of chicken or pork braised in soy sauce, vinegar, garlic, bay leaves and peppercorns.
steps: Marinate chicken in soy sauce, smashed garlic and pepper for at least 30 minutes.
Heat oil and sear marinated chicken until lightly browned on all sides.
Add the marinade, vinegar, water, bay leaves and brown sugar without stirring.
Bring to a boil, then reduce and simmer uncovered for 30 minutes until chicken is tender.
Remove chicken and reduce sauce until glossy and slightly thickened.
Return chicken to the pan, coat in sauce and serve with steamed rice.

recipes.title: Sinigang
recipes.description: Filipino sour soup with pork, vegetables and tamarind broth.
steps: Boil pork ribs with quartered onion and tomatoes in water for 45 minutes, skimming foam.
Boil tamarind in 1 cup water until soft, then mash and strain pulp into the soup.
Add daikon and simmer 5 minutes until tender.
Add eggplant, long beans and okra, simmer until just cooked.
Season with fish sauce and add chillies.
Add water spinach last and cook 1 minute until wilted before serving.

recipes.title: Lechon
recipes.description: Whole roasted suckling pig with crackling skin, a centerpiece of Filipino fiestas.
steps: Pat the pig dry inside and out, then rub the cavity with salt, pepper, soy sauce and calamansi.
Stuff the cavity with lemongrass, smashed garlic and bay leaves; sew closed.
Truss the pig onto a spit and let air-dry uncovered, ideally overnight in the fridge.
Roast slowly over hot coals or in a 160C oven for 4 hours, basting occasionally.
Brush skin with oil and a little vinegar to crisp, then raise heat for 20 minutes until skin blisters.
Rest 20 minutes, then chop into pieces and serve with liver sauce made from pate, vinegar and aromatics.

recipes.title: Lumpia
recipes.description: Filipino spring rolls filled with seasoned ground pork and vegetables, fried until crisp.
steps: Saute chopped onion and garlic in a little oil, add pork and brown.
Add finely chopped carrot and cabbage, season with soy sauce and pepper, cook 3 minutes.
Cool the filling completely.
Place a heaped tablespoon of filling on a wrapper, fold in sides and roll tightly, sealing with beaten egg.
Heat oil to 170C and fry lumpia in batches for 4 minutes until golden and crisp.
Drain on paper towels and serve with sweet chilli sauce.

recipes.title: Pancit
recipes.description: Filipino stir-fried noodles with chicken, pork, shrimp and vegetables, often served at celebrations.
steps: Saute garlic and onion in oil, add diced chicken and pork, cook until browned.
Add shrimp and cook 1 minute until pink.
Pour in stock, soy sauce and oyster sauce; bring to a simmer.
Add carrot and noodles, tossing until they soften and absorb most of the liquid.
Add cabbage and snow peas, toss for 2 minutes until crisp-tender.
Serve hot with calamansi halves on the side.

recipes.title: Kare-Kare
recipes.description: Filipino oxtail and vegetable stew in a thick peanut sauce, served with sauteed shrimp paste.
steps: Simmer oxtail and tripe in water for 2.5 hours until very tender; reserve broth.
Steep annatto seeds in 100 ml hot water for 10 minutes, then strain.
Saute garlic and onion in oil, add annatto water, peanut butter and ground rice.
Pour in 1 liter reserved broth and whisk until sauce thickens.
Add oxtail and tripe back to the sauce and simmer 10 minutes to meld flavors.
Blanch eggplant, long beans, banana blossom and water spinach, then arrange on top.
Saute bagoong with a little garlic and onion until fragrant; serve alongside the stew.

recipes.title: Tinola
recipes.description: Filipino chicken soup with green papaya, ginger and chilli leaves in a clear ginger broth.
steps: Saute ginger, garlic and onion in oil until fragrant.
Add chicken pieces and brown lightly.
Pour in water and bring to a boil, skimming foam.
Simmer covered for 30 minutes until chicken is tender.
Add cubed green papaya and cook 8 minutes until just soft.
Season with fish sauce and pepper, then stir in chilli leaves and serve.

recipes.title: Bicol Express
recipes.description: Spicy Filipino pork stew from Bicol simmered in coconut milk with chillies and shrimp paste.
steps: Render diced pork belly in a pot until lightly browned and fat is released.
Add chopped onion, garlic and ginger, saute until soft.
Stir in shrimp paste and cook 1 minute until fragrant.
Pour in coconut milk and simmer covered for 30 minutes.
Add sliced long green chillies and bird's eye chillies, cook 10 more minutes.
Stir in coconut cream and reduce until thick and oily, season with pepper and serve.

recipes.title: Halo-Halo
recipes.description: Filipino shaved ice dessert layered with sweet beans, jellies, fruits, leche flan and ube ice cream.
steps: Layer 2 tbsp each of sweetened beans, plantains, jackfruit, macapuno, coconut gel and sago in tall glasses.
Sprinkle each glass with sugar.
Mound shaved ice high above the rim of each glass.
Drizzle generously with evaporated milk.
Top each glass with a slice of leche flan, a scoop of ube ice cream and a sprinkle of pinipig.
Serve immediately with a long spoon for mixing it all together.

recipes.title: Pinakbet
recipes.description: Ilocano vegetable stew of bitter melon, eggplant, squash and okra flavored with bagoong shrimp paste.
steps: Render pork belly in a wide pan until golden and fat is released.
Add garlic, onion, ginger and tomatoes, cook until tomatoes break down.
Stir in shrimp paste and cook 1 minute until fragrant.
Pour in water and add squash; simmer 5 minutes.
Layer in long beans, eggplant, bitter melon and okra without stirring.
Cover and simmer 8 minutes, then gently fold to combine and serve.

recipes.title: Chicken Inasal
recipes.description: Bacolod-style grilled chicken marinated in calamansi, vinegar, lemongrass, ginger and annatto oil.
steps: Crush lemongrass, ginger and garlic into a paste.
Marinate chicken with the paste, calamansi, vinegar, soy sauce, sugar and pepper for 4 hours.
Steep annatto seeds in warm oil for 15 minutes, then strain to make annatto oil.
Heat grill to medium-high and grill chicken for 8 minutes per side, basting with annatto oil.
Continue cooking until juices run clear and skin is charred.
Brush with more annatto oil and serve with garlic rice and dipping sauce of vinegar, calamansi and chillies.

recipes.title: Sisig
recipes.description: Sizzling chopped pork face and ears with onions, chillies and calamansi served on a hot plate.
steps: Boil pork face and ears with bay leaves, peppercorns and salt for 1 hour until tender.
Cool, then grill or broil until skin is crisp; chop very finely.
Saute garlic and onion in oil, add liver and cook briefly, then chop fine and return to pan.
Add chopped pork, soy sauce, calamansi and chillies; toss to combine.
Stir in mayonnaise off the heat for creaminess.
Transfer to a hot sizzling plate, crack eggs on top and let the heat cook them slightly before serving.

recipes.title: Pork Humba
recipes.description: Visayan-style braised pork belly with banana blossoms, soy, vinegar and palm sugar.
steps: Sear cubed pork belly in its own fat until golden brown.
Add chopped onion and garlic, cook until soft.
Pour in soy sauce, vinegar and water without stirring; add bay leaves, peppercorns and star anise.
Simmer covered for 1 hour until pork is tender.
Add brown sugar, black beans and rehydrated banana blossoms.
Continue cooking uncovered until sauce reduces to a glossy glaze.

recipes.title: Bistek
recipes.description: Filipino beef steak braised in soy sauce, calamansi and onions until tender.
steps: Slice beef thinly across the grain.
Marinate beef in soy sauce, calamansi, garlic and pepper for 1 hour.
Slice onions into rings and fry briefly in oil until just softened, then set aside.
Sear marinated beef in batches in the same pan over high heat until browned.
Pour marinade and water back in, add sugar and simmer 20 minutes until tender.
Top with the reserved onion rings and serve with steamed rice.

recipes.title: Laing
recipes.description: Bicolano dish of dried taro leaves stewed in coconut milk with chillies and shrimp paste.
steps: Render diced pork belly in a pot until golden.
Add garlic, onion and ginger; saute until fragrant.
Stir in shrimp paste and dried shrimp, cook 1 minute.
Pour in coconut milk and add dried taro leaves; do not stir to avoid itchiness.
Simmer gently for 35 minutes until leaves are tender and most liquid is absorbed.
Stir in coconut cream and chillies, cook 5 minutes more until oil separates.

recipes.title: Tortang Talong
recipes.description: Filipino eggplant omelette of grilled eggplant dipped in beaten egg and pan-fried until golden.
steps: Grill or roast eggplants over an open flame until skin is charred and flesh is soft.
Cool, peel off skin keeping the stem on, then flatten with a fork.
Saute onion and garlic, add pork and tomato, season with soy and pepper, cook until dry.
Beat eggs with salt in a wide plate.
Dip a flattened eggplant into the egg, place in hot oil and spoon some pork on top, then more egg.
Fry until golden on both sides and serve hot with banana ketchup or rice.
